text: B.J. Fogg's Persuasive Technology Lab at Stanford produced many of Silicon Valley's most influential designers, including Instagram's Mike Krieger. Fogg's Behavior Model holds that behavior occurs when motivation, ability, and a trigger converge at the same moment. Platforms engineered for engagement work by maximizing all three simultaneously: notifications supply the trigger, simplified interfaces lower the ability barrier, and variable rewards — the unpredictable mix of likes and new posts — exploit the motivational circuitry that drives slot-machine gambling. Fogg himself has expressed discomfort with how his research was applied at scale, noting that academic models of persuasion were never designed for deployment on billions of people.
question: According to Fogg's Behavior Model as described in the passage, when does behavior occur?

options.text: When a person has sufficient motivation and receives a compelling social reward
When motivation, ability, and a trigger converge at the same moment
When repeated exposure gradually reduces resistance to an action
When an interface removes all friction from a desired user action
